basics.name: Dhruv Haldar
basics.label: Aerospace Engineer
basics.summary: An Aerospace Engineer currently working in CFD

education.institution: KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm, Sweden
courses: Control Theory & Practice, Optimization & Systems Engineering, Lightweight Structures & FEM, Human Spaceflight, Space Physics,  Corporate Finance

awards.title: Venture Cup Sverige - Region Öst
awards.summary: Flemingsberg Science Award is for ideas that contribute to a better world. In addition to being named as one of Sweden’s top 20 best ideas, we received SEK 5000 prize money and an office in Flemingsberg Science innovation hub for three months.

awards.title: French - German Quiz
awards.summary: From Current Affairs to History, the quiz covered almost all the aspects crucial for both of the countries. Basically, it gave an overall representation of the past and the future relations between both of them . The quiz was divided into three rounds+1 bonus round, there were 5 teams with each of them having two members.

publications.name: Implementation of Wing Ice Protection System in More Electric Aircraft Technologies
publications.publisher: International Council of the Aeronautical Sciences (ICAS)
publications.author: Dhruv Haldar and Emil Holmgren and Andreas Johansson
publications.summary: More Electric Aircraft (MEA) technologies aim at reducing greenhouse gas emissions to make local and global air transport easier. This paper discusses the development of a tool to evaluate the Wing Ice Protection System (WIPS) power and fuel consumption of MEA airliners of any size and flight configuration.

publications.name: More Electric Aircraft (MEA)-Scaling Aspects and Weight Impact
publications.publisher: International Council of the Aeronautical Sciences (ICAS)
publications.author: Emil Holmgren and Dhruv Haldar and Lina Bertling Tjernberg and Andreas Johansson
publications.summary: Authored by Emil Holmgren, this paper is about investigating the fuel consumption of passenger aircraft with conventional bleed-air driven Environmental Control System (ECS) and aircraft with equivalent electrical systems. A framework has been developed in MATLAB to run simulations under various conditions to study the passenger-scaling effect and impact of subsystem weight.

interests.name: Computational Fluid Dynamics
keywords: Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) for Aerospace (Airfoil) and Automobiles (TUM DrivAer model, Ahmed Body)

references.name: Professor Lina Bertling Tjernberg
references.reference: Lina Bertling Tjernberg (Docent, Ph.D., MSc.) is a Professor in Power Grid Technology at KTH (The Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm, Sweden). She is the Director of the KTH Energy Platform, which coordinates and supports new research initiatives within the energy areas. She is a Fellow of IVA the Royal Swedish Academy of Engineering.

projects.name: Quantum Computing
projects.summary: Quantum computing is the use of quantum-mechanical phenomena such as superposition and entanglement to perform computation. Computers that perform quantum computations are known as quantum computers.
